steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence lists three key years, each with a when-clause containing internal commas. This is a complex series. The rule: when series items contain internal commas, use semicolons between items — including a semicolon before "and" before the final item.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — released; and: places a semicolon before "and" to introduce the third item, following the complex series rule correctly. ✓
  • Choice B — released and: omits the required semicolon, blurring the boundary between the second and third items. ✗
  • Choice C — released. And: creates an unwarranted sentence boundary, leaving the final list item as a fragment. ✗
  • Choice D — released, and: uses a comma instead of the semicolon needed to separate items in a complex series. ✗
That matches Choice A — released; and.
